title: Federalism: Example Matrix
lead: One matrix, three lenses — the same policy area can be ==cooperative, competitive and combative== at once. Use these as ready examples in any federalism answer.

head: Area
Cooperative
Competitive
Combative
rows: NITI indices: Health, SDG, School Education Quality, Water, Innovation, Export Preparedness
==Boycotts of NITI meetings== alleging fiscal centralisation
==GST Council (Art 279A)== — joint rate-setting; best example of fiscal cooperation
States compete on compliance and revenue mobilisation
Compensation disputes; [[Mohit Minerals]]: Council recommendations are ==persuasive, not binding==
CSS — Jal Jeevan, PMAY, SBM, PMGSY, NHM cost-sharing
Speed and coverage competition among states
Centre frames reforms; states implement single-windows
Resistance to NEP, entrance frameworks, ==language policy==
==One Nation One Ration Card==, DBT, Aadhaar integration
TN social welfare, Kerala HD model, Odisha disaster management
e-NAM, PM-KISAN data sharing, crop insurance
Renewables capacity, EV policies, green investment
==Inter-state river disputes==: Cauvery, Krishna, SYL, Mahanadi
==Assent delays==, floor-test controversies, reservation of bills
Delhi competes on health, education, transport
Policing reforms, conviction rates, cybercrime capacity
States ==withdrawing general consent to CBI==; alleged misuse
Fiscal discipline, own-tax revenue, capex competition
==Cesses outside the divisible pool==, borrowing limits
Eighth Schedule, classical languages, cultural grants
